text: MarketReel — Global Film DistributionDeadpoolFilm Synopsis & Narrative AnalysisMarketReel Intelligence Document 2016 Prepared for Acquisition ReviewProduction OverviewTitleDeadpoolYear2016DirectorTim MillerGenreSuperhero / Action-ComedyRuntime108 minStudio / Distributor20th Century Fox / MarvelPrincipal CastRyan Reynolds, Morena Baccarin, T.J. Miller, Ed Skrein, Stefan KapičićExecutive SynopsisMercenary Wade Wilson undergoes an experimental mutant procedure that leaves him disfigured but with regenerative healing powers, launching a brutal and comedic campaign of revenge.Detailed Narrative SynopsisDeadpool follows Wade Wilson, a wisecracking mercenary with a tragic backstory. Living modestly in New York, he falls deeply in love with Vanessa Carlysle, a sex worker with a similarly dark past. Their relationship is shattered when Wade is diagnosed with terminal cancer and, desperate to survive, he secretly volunteers for an experimental mutant-making program run by Ajax (Francis Freeman).The program subjects Wade to extreme torture under the guise of activating dormant mutant genes. It works — Wade gains a powerful regenerative healing ability — but the process disfigures him horrifically. Ajax, revealing he has no intention of curing Wade's cancer or his appearance, abandons him in a burning building. Wade barely survives, adopting the costumed alter ego "Deadpool."Breaking the fourth wall constantly, Deadpool narrates his own revenge story with self-aware humor, pop culture references, and gleefully graphic violence. He tracks down Ajax, aided reluctantly by X-Men trainees Colossus and Negasonic Teenage Warhead, who urge him toward heroism. Deadpool's primary motivation remains personal — rescuing Vanessa, who Ajax has kidnapped as leverage.The climax takes place on a decommissioned aircraft carrier, where Deadpool battles Ajax in a brutal showdown. Despite Colossus's pleas for mercy, Deadpool kills Ajax, declaring that superhero codes of conduct don't apply to him. He reunites with Vanessa, disfigured face and all, in a darkly romantic conclusion.Narrative Themes for Distribution AnalysisAnti-hero subversion of genre normsDark romance and loyaltyTrauma and identity reinventionMeta-commentary on pop cultureRevenge as catharsisTone & Genre ClassificationGenre Classification: Superhero / Action-ComedyAudience Rating Profile: See Censorship & Certification Document for territory-specific ratings.Narrative Complexity: This document should be read in conjunction with the Cultural Sensitivity Report and Marketing Strategy Brief for complete acquisition analysis.MarketReel Confidential — Deadpool Synopsis Document — For Internal Distribution Use Only
